stimulus: In the historical novel The Inheritance of Loss, Indian American author Kiran Desai uses shifting narrators rather than a single perspective ______blank the complicated histories of a community in northeastern India.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The convention being tested is the use of finite and nonfinite verb forms within a sentence. The main clause "Desai uses shifting narrators rather than a single perspective" already has the finite verb "uses," so the blank requires a nonfinite form. The to-infinitive "to narrate" forms an infinitive clause that expresses purpose, explaining why Desai uses shifting narrators.

Choice A is incorrect because the present participle "narrating" would form a participial modifier that, lacking a comma and a clear noun to modify, attaches awkwardly and does not convey purpose. Choice B is incorrect because the past participle "narrated" cannot stand as a purpose modifier; it would require an auxiliary to function as a finite verb. Choice D is incorrect because the finite present-tense verb "narrates" would create a second main verb without a coordinator, producing a run-on.
